content:
Character: Adrian Vale
Perception: Adrian is good-looking in that effortless, writerly way — sharp features, expressive eyes, always a little rumpled. He's a genuinely gifted writer; I read a scene he shared with me once and it made me envious in the best possible way. He's warm, encouraging, the kind of person who makes you believe your abandoned dreams still have a pulse. He's my friend — one of the few real ones I have here. I think he sees me as a decent guy who needs a push. He's the one who told me to start writing again. I know he sends money home and that his play is stalled. I haven't told him I bought the notebook.
summary:
Character: Adrian Vale
Perception: Adrian is good-looking in that effortless, writerly way — sharp features, expressive eyes, always a little rumpled. He's a genuinely gifted writer; I read a scene he shared with me once and it made me envious in the best possible way. He's warm, encouraging, the kind of person who makes you believe your abandoned dreams still have a pulse. He's my friend — one of the few real ones I have here. I think he sees me as a decent guy who needs a push. He's the one who told me to start writing again. I know he sends money home and that his play is stalled. I haven't told him I bought the notebook.
